macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. markets calm as S&P 500 futures edge higher amid bond-market inflation worries
items.summary: U.S. equity futures were modestly higher, with S&P 500 futures up 0.24%, while European equities were mixed to slightly positive. The market tone was shaped by a warning from the bond market over inflation and broader macro uncertainty, including geopolitical risk.

items.title: Federal Reserve calendar shows key remarks and data releases for the U.S. economy
items.summary: Today’s economic calendar includes multiple Fed-related events and important U.S. indicators, including a speech from Fed Governor Waller and several housing, labor, inflation, and confidence releases. These data points are likely to help shape expectations for the Fed’s next policy steps.

items.title: Fed Governor Barr highlights consumer financial health metrics
items.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Michael Barr delivered remarks on consumer financial health metrics, underscoring the Fed’s interest in how financial innovation can affect consumer outcomes. The speech points to continued Fed focus on household balance sheets and financial stability.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin slides to around $76K as liquidations and ETF outflows hit crypto markets
items.summary: Crypto markets saw a sharp pullback over the past 24 hours, with Bitcoin falling to the mid-$76,000 range amid heavy liquidations and significant ETF outflows. Ether and XRP also weakened as investors reacted to broader risk-off sentiment.

items.title: CLARITY Act advances in Congress, boosting crypto regulatory momentum
items.summary: The CLARITY Act cleared a key committee hurdle, marking one of the biggest recent legislative steps for U.S. crypto market structure reform. The bill's progress comes as lawmakers continue debating how to oversee digital assets and token markets.

items.title: South Carolina signs pro-crypto, anti-CBDC bill into law
items.summary: South Carolina enacted legislation supportive of cryptocurrency while rejecting central bank digital currency (CBDC) frameworks. The move adds to the growing patchwork of state-level crypto policy initiatives in the U.S.

items.title: Tether buys SoftBank out of bitcoin treasury company XXI
items.summary: Tether acquired SoftBank's stake in XXI, a bitcoin-focused treasury company, strengthening its exposure to bitcoin-linked corporate holdings. The deal highlights continued institutional consolidation around bitcoin treasury strategies.

items.title: Trump Media withdraws SEC registration for Truth Social bitcoin ETFs
items.summary: Trump Media pulled its SEC registration for proposed Truth Social bitcoin ETFs, pausing a notable crypto product filing. The withdrawal comes amid an active but uncertain regulatory environment for new crypto-linked investment vehicles.

items.title: HIVE Digital Technologies unveils $3.5B AI gigafactory plan in Toronto
items.summary: HIVE Digital Technologies announced a major $3.5 billion AI gigafactory project in Toronto, expanding its infrastructure ambitions beyond crypto mining. The project underscores how some blockchain-native firms are pivoting toward AI and data-center growth.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google unveils Gemini Omni, a new multimodal AI model family focused on video creation and editing
items.summary: Google introduced Gemini Omni at Google I/O, describing it as a model family for multimodal creation and editing across text, images, video, and some audio. The launch positions Gemini Omni as a new foundation for interactive media generation and editing inside the Gemini app.

items.title: Google adds Gemini 3.5 Flash and says it is optimized for speed and efficiency
items.summary: Google also announced Gemini 3.5 Flash, highlighting it as a fast and capable model with strong token throughput. The model is being presented as a lighter-weight option for applications that need lower latency and high output speed.

items.title: Google introduces 'anti-gravity' agent tools aimed at multi-agent orchestration
items.summary: In the same set of announcements, Google previewed anti-gravity tooling focused on core agent conversations, produced artifacts, and multi-agent orchestration. The emphasis signals continued investment in agent-first workflows for developers and end users.

items.title: Gemini Omni is rolling into the Gemini app for paid subscribers
items.summary: Google said Gemini Omni is available in the Gemini app for paid subscribers, where it can combine text, images, and video inputs to generate new content. The company is framing this as a consumer-facing step toward richer multimodal editing and creation.

items.title: Report highlights growing use of AI in U.S. military operations and targeting
items.summary: A recent report says the Pentagon is accelerating AI adoption across defense systems, including targeting and intelligence workflows, as part of a push toward an AI-driven battlefield. It also notes ongoing criticism from civil liberties and safety advocates over autonomous weapons and surveillance risks.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Rumors point to a Nintendo Direct by mid-June
items.summary: Multiple gaming reports cited in recent coverage suggest Nintendo is preparing a Direct presentation for sometime by the middle of June. It is not yet clear whether it would be a full Direct or a partner showcase.

items.title: PlayStation schedules a State of Play for June 2
items.summary: Sony has announced a State of Play event set for June 2, described as an hour-long show with extended looks at upcoming games. The announcement is already driving speculation about major reveals and release-date updates.

items.title: GTA 6 watch continues ahead of Take-Two earnings call
items.summary: Coverage around Grand Theft Auto 6 says Rockstar has not posted any new announcement, pushing attention to Take-Two's earnings call on May 21. Fans are watching for a possible trailer, pre-order news, or confirmation of the game's launch window.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 draws attention after strong launch numbers
items.summary: Recent gaming coverage highlights Forza Horizon 6 as a standout performer, with discussion focused on its launch momentum and platform availability. It is being cited as one of the bigger releases generating player interest this week.

items.title: Adventures of Elliot demo goes live
items.summary: A new demo for Adventures of Elliot is now available, giving players an early look at the game ahead of release. The demo launch is one of the notable smaller release updates in current gaming coverage.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Google unveils new Android XR smartglasses tease at I/O
items.summary: Google and Samsung teased upcoming Android XR smartglasses during Google I/O, signaling continued momentum in wearable computing beyond phones and headsets. The devices are part of Google’s broader push into spatial computing and mixed reality.

items.title: Plex says its lifetime subscription price will triple to $750
items.summary: Plex announced a major pricing change for its premium lifetime plan, which will rise to $750 on June 30. The move is likely to affect long-time users considering a one-time purchase.

items.title: Samsung announces new displays including a 6K gaming monitor
items.summary: Samsung introduced a new lineup of monitors, highlighted by what it says is the world’s first 6K gaming monitor. The company also unveiled a larger version of its MovingStyle display.

items.title: Spotify adds verification badges for podcasts
items.summary: Spotify is launching verification badges for podcasts as it works to help listeners distinguish authentic shows from low-quality or misleading content. The feature follows a similar verification effort the company recently introduced for music.

items.title: Apple previews accessibility updates for Vision Pro and other devices
items.summary: Apple is previewing a new wave of accessibility features, including updates that let Vision Pro users control a wheelchair with their eyes. The company also highlighted Apple Intelligence-powered accessibility tools such as natural-language voice input.

items.title: Meta reportedly reassigns 7,000 employees to AI-focused roles
items.summary: Meta is said to be moving thousands of employees into new AI-centered teams as it reorganizes its internal structure. The changes reflect the company’s continued prioritization of AI product development and infrastructure.
